title: Convert report implementation to a skill
description: Refactor and package the report implementation as a reusable skill, including wiring, docs, and validation coverage.

title: Fix transitive agent→website crossing: agent tool reaches web store via penny.reminders
description:
# Fix transitive agent→website crossing: agent tool reaches the web store via penny.reminders

## Background (why this matters)

Penny has a HARD CONSTRAINT (see AGENTS.local.md, "Architectural segregation: agent vs website"): the agent domain (`penny/tools/`, `tools/_services/`, `.agent/skills/`, `agent_factory.py`) must have **zero** imports of website/app persistence or CRUD code. The dependency rule is one-directional: Website → Agent only. App-owned data (conversations, reminders, onboarding) lives in the website's own persistence package (`penny/api/persistence/`, separate `WebBase`/engine/schema) precisely so the agent can never read or mutate it.

`tests/test_domain_segregation.py` enforces this with an AST import scan — but only for **direct** imports of `penny.api.persistence` / `penny.billing` from `penny/tools` and the skills tree.

## The leak

There is a **transitive** crossing the guardrail misses:

- `backend/penny/tools/_services/plaid_link.py` (imports near the top, ~lines 27–31) imports `penny.reminders.DbReminderQueue`.
- `backend/penny/reminders.py` (~lines 23–25) imports `penny.api.persistence.models.QueuedReminder` and `penny.api.persistence.tenant.owner_web_session` — i.e. the website store.

Net effect: an **agent tool service writes the website database** (it enqueues a reminder in the web store's `queued_reminders` table). That is exactly what the hard constraint forbids — agent code touching app-owned persistence — and it slips through CI because the import chain has one intermediate hop (`penny.reminders` is not on the denylist and the scan is not transitive).

(Line numbers are from branch `refactor/stabilize-architecture` circa 2026-07; they may have drifted — grep for `DbReminderQueue` and `api.persistence` to locate current sites.)

Note the injection seam that already exists and shows the intended design: `build_agent(..., reminders: ReminderQueue | None)` in `backend/penny/agent_factory.py` (~lines 220–224) takes the agent-harness `ReminderQueue` **Protocol**, and the DB-backed implementation is injected by the caller (`api/main.py` passes `DbReminderQueue`) "to preserve segregation". The `_services/plaid_link.py` import bypasses that seam by constructing the concrete web-backed queue directly inside the agent domain.

## Fix approach

1. Remove the `penny.reminders` import from `tools/_services/plaid_link.py`. Options, in rough order of preference:
   - Thread the already-injected `ReminderQueue` Protocol down to the service (the tool layer receives it via injection, mirroring how `agent_factory` already accepts it), so the agent domain only ever sees the harness Protocol; or
   - Move the "enqueue a reminder after Plaid link" step out of the agent service entirely — have the website side (the caller of the link flow, e.g. the `/api/plaid/exchange` route in `api/main.py`) enqueue it after the service returns.
2. Move the DB-backed implementation `penny/reminders.py` (which is website code by dependency: it imports the web store) into the website domain — e.g. under `penny/api/` or a website-owned peer module — leaving only Protocol usage on the agent side. Update the import in `api/main.py` accordingly.
3. Harden the guardrail so this class of leak can't recur: in `backend/tests/test_domain_segregation.py`, either make the import scan transitive (follow first-party import edges from `penny/tools` + skills and assert the closure never reaches `penny.api.persistence`/`penny.billing`) or, minimally, add the moved reminders module to the denylist. Prefer the transitive check — the point of this bug is that a one-hop indirection defeated the direct scan.

## Acceptance criteria

- No module under `penny/tools/` (including `_services/`) or `.agent/skills/` imports, directly or transitively (first-party closure), `penny.api.persistence` or `penny.billing`.
- The reminder-after-Plaid-link behaviour still works (reminder row created in the web store after a successful link) — verify via the existing tests around plaid link / reminders, or add one.
- The DB-backed reminder queue lives in website-owned code; the agent domain references only the agent-harness `ReminderQueue` Protocol.
- `tests/test_domain_segregation.py` updated so that reintroducing the old import chain fails CI (demonstrate by temporarily re-adding the import if practical).
- Verification gate passes: `cd backend && uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check . && uv run pytest -q`.

## Related

- A sibling bead covers `api/main.py` importing `tools/_services/plaid_link.exchange_public_token` directly (website→agent direction, allowed but reaching into `_services` internals). Both touch `plaid_link`; coordinate if worked concurrently, but they are independently landable.

title: Establish parity between Transactoid CLI and MCP implementations
description: Ensure feature and behavior parity between the Transactoid CLI and MCP implementations. Include a parity checklist, gap closure, and regression safeguards so both surfaces evolve together.
acceptance_criteria: CLI and MCP implementations expose matching capabilities and behavior. A maintained parity checklist exists and is used during changes. Local agent memory explicitly states CLI and MCP must remain in parity.

title: Expose tool for cloud storage uploads
description: Add and document tool support for uploading artifacts to configured cloud storage. Email send and local disk write tooling are already implemented and are out of scope for this issue.
acceptance_criteria: Cloud upload tool interface exists and is wired into runtime/tool registry with docs and validation tests. Security and permission boundaries for cloud upload are documented.

title: Clean up Graphite instructions in AGENTS.local.md and perform general cleanup pass
description: Review and simplify the Graphite workflow guidance in AGENTS.local.md for clarity and correctness, then do a focused cleanup pass for nearby local-agent guidance to reduce drift and ambiguity.
acceptance_criteria: Graphite section in AGENTS.local.md is concise, current, and unambiguous. Redundant or stale nearby guidance is removed or rewritten. Resulting instructions are internally consistent and easier to follow.

title: Low-priority cleanup: noqa, TYPE_CHECKING, and nested non-CLI imports
description: Perform a cleanup pass to remove or justify noqa usage where relevant, simplify or correct if TYPE_CHECKING patterns, and eliminate nested imports in non-CLI implementations where top-level imports are appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: Unnecessary noqa directives are removed or documented. if TYPE_CHECKING usage is consistent and purposeful. Nested imports in non-CLI code paths are reduced unless required for runtime constraints. Changes preserve behavior and pass lint/type/test checks.

title: Improve agent prompt output per agent loop prompt
description: Improve the agent prompt output format and quality to align with the agent loop prompt expectations, including clearer structure, consistency, and actionable responses.
acceptance_criteria: Agent prompt outputs conform to agent loop prompt requirements. Output structure and wording are more consistent and actionable. Relevant tests and/or prompt validations cover the updated behavior.

title: Update Fly.io jobs to run skill-based spending reports
description: Update Fly.io scheduled jobs so spending reports are generated via skills for four cadences: daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly.
acceptance_criteria: Fly.io has scheduled jobs for: daily spending report (skill-based), weekly spending report (skill-based), monthly spending report (skill-based), and yearly spending report (skill-based). Job schedules, invocation wiring, and required runtime configuration are documented and validated.

title: Front doors import agent-internal tools/_services — add a declared facade
description:
# Give front doors a declared facade instead of importing agent-internal tools/_services

## Background (why this matters)

Penny's layering convention (AGENTS.md "Conventions" / "Design rules"): agent-facing tool wrappers live in `penny/tools/*.py` and their implementations in `penny/tools/_services/` — the underscore marks that package as **agent-domain private**. The website→agent dependency direction is allowed (AGENTS.local.md hard constraint), but front doors depending on the *internal layout* of `_services` couples them to a private module tree: any restructuring of the agent's service internals breaks the web API and CLI, and it undermines a future extraction of the agent into its own package (the `_services` tree would become published API by accident).

## The leaks

Two front doors import `penny.tools._services` internals directly:

1. `backend/penny/api/main.py` (~line 329): `from penny.tools._services.plaid_link import exchange_public_token` — used by the `POST /api/plaid/exchange` route (~lines 318–352), which calls the service directly with a finance DB session, bypassing the agent entirely.
2. `backend/penny/cli.py` (~lines 307–308): imports `penny.tools._services.sync_service.SyncTool` for the `sync` command.

(Line numbers from branch `refactor/stabilize-architecture` circa 2026-07; grep for `tools._services` outside `penny/tools/` to find current sites.)

These are the **only** `_services` reaches from outside the agent domain — everything else goes through `build_agent`/`build_model` (`penny/agent_factory.py`) or the harness event stream via `api/bridge.py`.

## Fix approach

Promote the handful of service entry points that front doors legitimately need into a small declared facade, keeping `_services` agent-private:

- `penny/services/` already exists as the per-process singleton facade (`get_taxonomy`, `get_persister`, `build_categorizer`, `get_migrator`, plus `services/email.py`, `services/scheduled_reports.py`). Add the two operations there (or a sibling module within `penny/services/`), e.g. re-export/wrap `exchange_public_token` and a sync entry point. Thin re-export is acceptable — the point is a *declared, stable* surface, not new abstraction; docstring should say this is the front-door seam.
- Update `api/main.py` and `cli.py` to import from the facade.
- Add a guardrail: extend `backend/tests/test_domain_segregation.py` (or add a test beside it) asserting nothing outside `penny/tools/` imports `penny.tools._services.*`. This makes the privacy of `_services` enforced rather than conventional.

Do NOT invert the direction (no agent code importing the front doors) and do not move the implementations out of `_services` if they are also used by agent tools — the facade wraps/re-exports; single source of truth stays in `_services`.

## Acceptance criteria

- No import of `penny.tools._services` anywhere outside `penny/tools/` (enforced by a new/extended guardrail test).
- `POST /api/plaid/exchange` and `penny sync` behave exactly as before (existing tests pass; exercise the route/command if coverage is thin).
- The facade module documents that it is the declared seam for front doors (web API, CLI) into finance service operations.
- Verification gate passes: `cd backend && uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check . && uv run pytest -q`.

## Related

- A sibling bead fixes `tools/_services/plaid_link.py` importing the web-backed reminder queue (an agent→website leak). Both touch `plaid_link`; independently landable, but coordinate if worked concurrently.
- Context: this facade also serves a possible future split of the agent into its own installable package — the facade would be part of its public API while `_services` stays private.

title: Expand list_plaid_accounts into four CRUD-specific tools
description: Replace or expand list_plaid_accounts into four distinct tools aligned to CRUD operations: create, read, update, and delete account-related actions.
acceptance_criteria: Four explicit Plaid account tools exist with clear responsibilities for create, read, update, and delete operations. Tool naming and interfaces are consistent with existing conventions. Documentation and validation tests cover each new tool path.

title: Add tax returns as untracked local data and a skill to review them
description: Add support for storing personal tax return files as untracked local data in the repository workspace, and implement a skill that can safely peruse and summarize those tax return documents.
acceptance_criteria: Tax return data path is configured as untracked/local-only and excluded from version control. A dedicated skill can access and peruse the tax return data for user-requested analysis. Privacy/security boundaries and usage docs are defined. Tests or validations cover core skill behavior.

title: Extract generic agent scaffolding into a reusable library
description:
Create a reusable library by extracting generic agent scaffolding from Transactoid. Scope includes UI surfaces (MCP, ChatKit, ACP, headless), multi-runtime interface and implementations, skill support across runtimes, memory abstractions, and policy guardrails.

Confirmed findings to include in scope:
- Multiple UI surfaces already exist and share orchestration concepts: src/transactoid/ui/mcp/server.py, src/transactoid/ui/chatkit/server.py, src/transactoid/ui/acp/server.py, plus headless execution in src/transactoid/services/agent_run/service.py and src/transactoid/jobs/report/runner.py.
- Runtime abstraction already exists via CoreRuntime protocol and event model in src/transactoid/core/runtime/protocol.py, with provider runtimes and factory wiring in src/transactoid/core/runtime/.
- Shared tool invocation and provider-specific tool adapters already exist and should be libraryized: src/transactoid/core/runtime/shared_tool_invoker.py and src/transactoid/core/runtime/tool_adapters/.
- Promptorium is already used for prompt loading/versioned keys and should be the standard prompt management path.
- Existing policy expectations should be encoded as reusable guardrails, including CLI/MCP parity and project-local planning artifacts.
- Report jobs already run headlessly and Fly.io job configuration exists; this should be generalized for cron-driven agent runs.
- Memory behavior currently differs by surface (session maps and in-memory stores), and memory work in the merchant-rules worktree should be integrated into a unified library memory model.
acceptance_criteria:
A standalone library package is produced with clear module boundaries for: runtimes, tool adapters/invocation, UI bindings, skills, memory, and policy enforcement. Transactoid consumes the library via thin adapters.

MCP, ChatKit, ACP, and headless surfaces are wired through shared library primitives with minimal app-specific code.

A runtime interface contract supports all providers with consistent run/session/event semantics and skill execution behavior across providers.

Skill support is standardized across runtimes (including Claude path) with parity tests and capability matrix.

Memory support is standardized with pluggable backends and consistent lifecycle semantics across UIs/runtimes; merchant-rules memory WIP is reconciled.

Policy mechanisms exist in code/tests for CLI/MCP parity checks, project-local plan artifact enforcement, Promptorium-based prompt management, and cron-compatible agent runs.

Fly.io/cron job wiring supports daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly skill-based report runs via the extracted scaffolding.

Migration plan is documented and staged so existing Transactoid behavior remains intact during extraction.

title: Inspect Langfuse runs and make improvements aggressively
description: Follow-up to Langfuse tracing (fly-mvj): once traces are flowing, regularly inspect real runs and aggressively act on what they reveal — prompt fixes, tool changes, categorizer tuning, latency/cost cuts. Treat it as an ongoing improvement loop driven by trace data.

title: Verify conversation persistence + chat history drawer end-to-end
description:
Make sure conversations are actually persisted and surfaced correctly, with end-to-end coverage.

Three parts:
1. Conversation persistence: verify conversations (and their messages) are written to the DB correctly during/after a chat session — right records, associations (user/conversation/message), and timing. Fix any gaps.
2. Chat history drawer: verify the drawer lists the persisted conversations (reads from website-owned persistence, ordering/labels correct), and that selecting one loads its messages.
3. E2E tests: add end-to-end tests covering the full loop — have a conversation -> it persists -> it appears in the history drawer -> reopening it renders the messages.

Domain: website/app. Conversation/message persistence + the list/fetch endpoints are website-owned (backend/penny/api/); no agent-domain involvement in reading history. Relates to fly-zzr (show conversation history UI).
acceptance_criteria:
- Conversations and messages are persisted correctly (verified against the DB).
- The chat history drawer lists persisted conversations; selecting one loads its messages.
- E2E tests cover: converse -> persists -> appears in drawer -> reopen renders history.
- E2E tests pass in CI/local and read from website-owned persistence only.

title: Run transactoid sync once per day
description: Add scheduling and operational wiring to execute transactoid sync automatically once per day.
acceptance_criteria: A daily schedule exists to run transactoid sync exactly once per day. Runtime configuration, secrets, and failure/retry behavior are documented. Execution is observable via logs and validated in deployment docs/tests.

title: Auto-update merchant rules on taxonomy rename/remove/merge
description: Implement support to automatically update merchant rules when taxonomy categories are renamed, removed, or merged, preserving rule validity and minimizing manual remediation.
acceptance_criteria: Merchant rules are automatically migrated/updated for taxonomy rename, remove, and merge operations. Invalid or unmappable rules are surfaced with actionable diagnostics. Behavior is covered by deterministic tests for each taxonomy mutation type.

title: Implement memory support for all recategorization actions
description: Add memory support so all recategorization actions are captured with consistent context and can be reused across future agent interactions.
acceptance_criteria: All recategorization actions persist relevant memory context. Memory retrieval is available for subsequent recategorization flows. Behavior is covered by deterministic tests and documented.

title: Add merchant rules for Morgan Stanley mortgage payments
description:
Add merchant rules to classify the following recurring Morgan Stanley automated payments as mortgage payments.

Observed transactions:
- 2026-02-02 | $10,861.79 | Automated Payment MORGAN STANLEY
- 2026-01-02 | $10,861.79 | Automated Payment MORGAN STANLEY LOAN PAYMT
- 2025-12-01 | $10,861.79 | Automated Payment MORGAN STANLEY LOAN PAYMT
- 2025-11-03 | $10,378.48 | Automated Payment MORGAN STANLEY
- 2025-10-01 | $10,378.48 | Automated Payment MORGAN STANLEY
- 2025-09-02 | $10,378.48 | Automated Payment MORGAN STANLEY
- Consistent $10,378.48 back to 2024-12
- 2024-11-01 | $10,457.33 | Automated Payment MORGAN STANLEY LOAN PAYMT
acceptance_criteria:
Merchant rules classify these Morgan Stanley automated payment descriptors as mortgage payments.

Rules handle both descriptor variants:
- "Automated Payment MORGAN STANLEY"
- "Automated Payment MORGAN STANLEY LOAN PAYMT"

Regression coverage verifies classification stays mapped to mortgage for representative examples across the provided date/amount ranges.

title: Ensure AGENTS.local.md requires Promptorium for prompt version management
description: Update AGENTS.local.md instructions so agents are explicitly required to use Promptorium for prompt versioning and prompt management workflows.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.local.md explicitly states Promptorium is required for prompt version management. Wording is clear and unambiguous for agent behavior. Any conflicting prompt-management guidance is reconciled.

title: [EPIC] ACP UI improvements
description: Track low-priority ACP UI improvement tasks.

title: Show return code 1 as failure in Toad UI via ACP adapters
description: Update ACP adapter/UI integration so process return code 1 is surfaced as a failure state in Toad UI.

title: Show execute_shell_command as terminal commands
description: Update ACP/Toad UI presentation so execute_shell_command tool calls render as terminal commands.

title: Pretty print run_sql queries and generate dynamic run_sql titles
description: In ACP/Toad UI results, pretty print SQL queries produced by run_sql, and make the run_sql display title generated dynamically from a tool argument instead of a static label.

title: Emit tool output for recategorize merchant tool
description: Add ACP UI/runtime support to emit and render tool output for the recategorize merchant tool so users can see actionable completion details in the tool output stream.
design: Wire recategorize merchant responses through the existing tool output emission path used by other ACP tools. Ensure output payloads include key result fields (for example target merchant, affected transaction count, and outcome status) and are presented consistently in the UI adapter.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Recategorize merchant tool emits tool output events on success and failure paths. 2) Output is surfaced in ACP UI with consistent formatting used by other tools. 3) Tests cover emitted payload shape and UI presentation behavior. 4) No regression for existing tool output handling.

title: Relax execute_shell_command write policy to allow arbitrary safe commands
description: Current execute_shell_command write policy is too strict and blocks normal shell usage (for example simple listing commands like ls). Replace the default allow-list model with a narrow disallow-list model so arbitrary commands are allowed by default except explicitly dangerous or destructive operations.
design: Implement policy evaluation as deny-overrides-allow with a small explicit deny list. Keep high-risk destructive operations blocked (for example filesystem wipe, git hard reset and restore variants, and privilege escalation patterns), but allow routine shell commands and common developer workflows without pre-enumeration.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Commands like ls, rg, cat, git status, and similar read-only or normal developer workflows are allowed without adding per-command exceptions. 2) Policy enforcement uses a narrow disallow list instead of a broad allow list. 3) Existing protections for clearly destructive commands remain in place and are covered by tests. 4) Add or adjust tests demonstrating allowed arbitrary safe commands and blocked dangerous commands.

title: Add analysis-only memory entries parallel to merchant-rules memory
description: Implement a dedicated analysis-only memory feature for entries used explicitly by analysis workflows (for example run_sql tool usage). This is separate from merchant-rules memory, which currently supports categorization and analysis together. The new feature should run in parallel to merchant-rules memory and should not change merchant-rule semantics.
design: Add a parallel memory path/model for analysis-only entries, with explicit routing from analysis workflows (including run_sql) to this store. Keep merchant-rules memory intact and isolated. Define clear boundaries: analysis-only memory is for analytical context and query assistance, while merchant-rules memory remains for categorization-oriented behavior (and any existing mixed usage).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Analysis workflows (including run_sql) can create/read/update analysis-only memory entries. 2) Analysis-only memory is stored and managed in a path/model that is distinct from merchant-rules memory. 3) Merchant-rules memory behavior remains unchanged. 4) Tests verify isolation between the two memory systems and correct analysis-only retrieval behavior. 5) Documentation explains when to use analysis-only memory vs merchant-rules memory.

title: Add budget support using agent memory
description: Implement budget support backed by agent memory so budget definitions, progress, and relevant context can be stored and retrieved across runs.
design: Introduce budget-focused memory structures and workflows in the agent memory layer, including creation, retrieval, updates, and progress tracking. Keep the implementation aligned with existing memory lifecycle patterns and ensure budget behavior is not coupled to unrelated categorization logic.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Users can create and persist budget definitions via agent memory. 2) Budget progress can be read and updated across runs. 3) Tests cover budget memory create/read/update flows and persistence behavior. 4) Documentation describes budget support and memory model expectations.

title: Define strategy to version control personal documents outside core repo
description: Design and implement a strategy for version controlling personal documents while keeping them out of the core Transactoid repository. The approach should preserve privacy and repo hygiene while still enabling reproducible document workflows.
design: Evaluate and document one or more separation patterns (for example separate private repo, submodule/subtree with strict boundaries, or synchronized external path) and define operational rules for local development, backup, and deployment contexts. Include ignore rules and guardrails to prevent accidental inclusion of personal documents in the core repo.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A concrete storage/versioning strategy is selected and documented. 2) Core repo is protected from accidental personal-document commits (ignore rules and/or policy checks). 3) Workflow steps are documented for create/update/sync/backup of personal documents. 4) Validation demonstrates personal docs remain outside tracked core repo files.

title: Fail startup when DB URL is unset; remove implicit SQLite fallback
description: Stop defaulting to SQLite when a database URL is not configured. The app should fail fast if DB URL is unset so development and production both use the intended real database.
design: Make DB URL configuration mandatory at startup/runtime entrypoints. Replace implicit SQLite fallback creation with explicit configuration validation and clear error messaging. Keep behavior aligned with the project's existing PostgreSQL coupling and assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: 1) App startup fails with a clear error when DB URL is missing. 2) No on-the-fly SQLite database creation occurs as a fallback. 3) Configuration/docs are updated to state DB URL is required and PostgreSQL is the expected backend. 4) Tests cover failure-on-missing-DB-URL and absence of SQLite fallback behavior.

title: Replace Amazon scrape-orders log spam with structured telemetry
description: Refactor the Amazon scrape orders tool to remove noisy log spam and replace it with proper telemetry that is actionable and queryable.
design: Introduce structured telemetry events/metrics for key scrape-orders lifecycle points (start, progress checkpoints, retries/errors, completion summary) and reduce unbounded repetitive logs. Keep user-facing output concise while preserving observability for debugging and operations.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Existing log spam in the Amazon scrape-orders tool is removed or significantly reduced. 2) Structured telemetry is emitted for major lifecycle events and failure modes. 3) Telemetry includes useful dimensions (for example account/source, page/order counts, duration, error category). 4) Tests or validation coverage confirm telemetry emission and non-spam logging behavior.

title: Create a tool for generating visualizations
description: Add a tool that generates visualizations from analysis outputs/data so users can quickly inspect trends and distributions.
design: Define a clear tool interface (input data/query, chart type/options, and output artifact format), implement rendering with deterministic defaults, and integrate with existing runtime/tool registration and artifact handling.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Tool can generate at least core visualization types (for example line, bar, and pie/chart alternatives as appropriate). 2) Inputs and configuration are validated with clear errors. 3) Output artifacts are produced in a consumable format and surfaced through existing output channels. 4) Tests cover tool invocation, validation, and artifact generation behavior.

title: Revive web support for ChatKit
description: Restore and modernize web support for ChatKit so agent interactions can be used reliably through a web interface.
design: Re-enable ChatKit web surface wiring, align with current runtime/tooling interfaces, and ensure parity with existing non-web interaction paths where applicable.
acceptance_criteria: 1) ChatKit web support is functional end-to-end. 2) Core interaction flows (session start, message exchange, tool/event rendering) work in web mode. 3) Tests/validation cover critical web ChatKit paths.

title: Host ChatKit web support in a deployable environment
description: Deploy the revived ChatKit web support to a hosted environment so users can access it without local setup.
design: Define deployment target, environment configuration, secrets management, and observability for hosted ChatKit web service.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Hosted environment is configured and reachable. 2) Deployment process is documented and repeatable. 3) Monitoring/logging and basic operational checks are in place.

title: Add report-email buttons to continue agent runs in web with predetermined prompts
description: Add actionable buttons in report emails that open the web experience and continue the agent run with predetermined follow-up questions when clicked.
design: Generate signed/deep-linkable actions in report emails that map to predefined prompt intents and resume/continue the relevant agent context in hosted web ChatKit flows.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Report emails include continuation buttons for predefined follow-up prompts. 2) Clicking a button opens hosted web experience and continues the run with the intended prompt. 3) Links are secure/validated and handle expired/invalid states gracefully. 4) Tests/validation cover link generation and continuation behavior.

title: ChatKit web revival and email continuation flow
description: Track revival of ChatKit web support, deploy hosting, and report-email continuation actions in a single roadmap epic.
acceptance_criteria: Child issues are linked under this epic and dependencies reflect delivery order.

title: Add Gmail integration to improve expense categorization fidelity
description: Integrate Gmail signals into categorization workflows to improve expense-category accuracy and reduce ambiguous classifications.
design: Add a Gmail ingestion path for relevant transactional/email metadata (for example receipts, merchant confirmations, and billing notices), map extracted signals into categorization features, and keep data handling privacy-conscious with clear opt-in and retention boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Gmail integration can ingest and normalize relevant expense-related messages. 2) Categorization flow can consume Gmail-derived signals to improve classification fidelity. 3) Privacy/security controls are documented (scopes, consent, retention). 4) Tests/validation demonstrate improved categorization outcomes on representative cases and no regression for existing categorization paths.

title: Add transaction deduplication support
description: Implement robust transaction deduplication to prevent duplicate records from ingestion and synchronization workflows.
design: Define dedupe keys and matching strategy (exact and near-duplicate handling), apply dedupe during ingest/sync paths, and provide safe conflict-handling for ambiguous matches.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Duplicate transactions are detected and not re-inserted in normal ingest/sync flows. 2) Dedupe logic is deterministic and documented. 3) Ambiguous cases are handled safely with clear logging/telemetry. 4) Tests cover exact duplicates, near-duplicates, and non-duplicate edge cases.

title: Add a LangGraph Deep Agents runtime
description: Implement a runtime integration for LangGraph Deep Agents so Transactoid can execute agent workflows through that runtime backend.
design: Add a new runtime adapter that conforms to existing runtime protocol contracts, including session/run lifecycle, tool invocation compatibility, and event streaming semantics.
acceptance_criteria: 1) LangGraph Deep Agents runtime is selectable and executable via existing runtime selection pathways. 2) Core lifecycle behavior (start, stream/events, completion/error) conforms to runtime protocol expectations. 3) Tool invocation compatibility and parity checks are covered by tests.

title: Add generic runtime validation for invalid model responses
description: Add shared validation across existing runtimes for generic invalid responses (for example empty LLM responses) to prevent silent failures and inconsistent handling.
design: Introduce common validation/guardrail logic at the runtime abstraction layer (or shared helper path) so all runtimes consistently detect and handle malformed/empty responses with clear errors and telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Existing runtimes consistently reject/handle empty or invalid model responses. 2) Error messages and telemetry are standardized across runtimes. 3) Tests cover validation behavior and non-regression for valid responses.

title: Add Claude runtime with built-in Claude tools on haiku-4.6
description: Implement a Claude runtime that supports built-in Claude tools and runs on model haiku-4.6.
design: Add Claude runtime wiring and configuration for haiku-4.6 with full built-in tool support, integrated with existing runtime interfaces, event flow, and tool lifecycle handling.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Claude runtime can be selected and executes on haiku-4.6. 2) Built-in Claude tools are available through runtime execution paths. 3) Tool/event behavior is covered by tests for success and failure paths. 4) Runtime docs/config clearly describe model/tool requirements.

title: Track all sessions and expose tool to record sessions as evals
description: Implement end-to-end session tracking across runtime interactions, and add a separate tool that can explicitly record session data as eval artifacts.
design: Add consistent session lifecycle tracking (creation, updates, completion, metadata) in core runtime/service paths, then expose a dedicated tool for turning selected sessions into eval records without coupling this to normal session persistence.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All sessions are tracked with consistent identifiers and lifecycle state. 2) A dedicated tool exists to record sessions as evals on demand. 3) Session tracking and eval-recording tool are decoupled and documented. 4) Tests cover session tracking completeness and eval-recording tool behavior.

title: Clean up taxonomy category names to match childcare naming quality
description: Standardize taxonomy category names across the project to match the clarity and consistency of the childcare categories.
design: Audit current taxonomy names, define naming rules based on the childcare category style (clear, human-readable, consistent granularity), and apply renames/mappings safely across dependent flows.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Taxonomy category names are reviewed and updated to align with childcare-style naming conventions. 2) Rename/migration mappings preserve behavior for existing rules and history. 3) Documentation captures naming conventions and examples. 4) Tests/validation confirm no regression in categorization behavior after renames.

title: Split agent and website into separate repos (open-core option): seam design + prerequisites
description:
Capture of the agent/website repo-split analysis (2026-07-05 session on refactor/stabilize-architecture). This is an option to keep ready, not scheduled work: what it would take to split the agent and the website/app into separate repos, with one public and one private.

## Verdict

Feasible; the seam mostly exists already. The agent repo becomes a Python package the website repo installs — the same pattern proven with agent-harness and agent-ui. The dependency direction is fixed (Website -> Agent, never reverse) and decides the public/private question: the agent can go public with the website private (open-core), but not the reverse — a public website that can't build without a private agent package is broken for outsiders, and the frontend is thoroughly Penny-specific anyway (Plaid card, billing/credits, invites, session modes).

## Repo boundary

- Agent repo ("penny-agent"): agent_factory, prompts.py + .prompts/, .agent/skills/, tools/ + tools/_services/, plugins/amazon/, services/, taxonomy/ + configs/taxonomy.yaml, rules/, normalizer/, memory/, workspace.py + workspace_store/, sandbox.py, config.py, security, errors, finance adapters/ (db, plaid, r2, amazon, cache), db.py, bootstrap.py, the tenancy kernel, observability/, cli.py, finance alembic migrations.
- Website repo: api/ (main, bridge, accumulator, hydration, persistence), billing/, signup.py, onboarding.py, the DB-backed reminders impl, adapters/clerk, frontend/, deploy/, web-schema migrations.
- Placement calls: cli.py ships with the agent (headless front door; keeps the cron contract `penny run-scheduled-report` intact and makes the public repo usable standalone via SQLite + dev principal). The tenancy kernel (RequestContext/SessionMode/ContextVar) lives in the agent repo and is exported — both domains need it and only Website -> Agent is allowed.

## The six seam contracts

1. One-directional package dependency: website pyproject depends on penny-agent (git tag or private index), with a [tool.uv.sources] editable path for local dev.
2. Construction API: exported build_agent(ctx, model, workspace_dir, usage_pricer, reminders, persist_session) + build_model(credential) + the injection Protocols (ReminderQueue, UsagePricer). Exists today at agent_factory.py; the split makes it a published, versioned API.
3. agent-harness types as the shared vocabulary at the boundary (events for bridge.py, Message for rehydrate, credential/pricer types). Neither repo owns them; don't invent Penny-specific boundary types.
4. Identity/provisioning facade replacing shared ORM imports — the biggest genuine gap. Today signup_routes.py / api/main.py import User/Household/PlaidItem from finance models and open finance-DB sessions directly. Export explicit functions instead: provision_household(...) (wraps bootstrap/seeding), get_household_profile(ctx), rename_household(ctx, name), plaid_item_status(ctx), exchange_public_token(...) (also fixes the main.py:329 reach into _services — fly-161).
5. Shared Postgres, split ownership: finance schema + RLS functions + their alembic env belong to the agent repo; web.* schema + its migrations to the website repo. Two version tables, two upgrade invocations at deploy. Today web migrations (019, 022, 023) sit in the finance tree — needs untangling.
6. PENNY_* env contract per repo: each declares its own .env.example; deploy (website repo) supplies values for both.

## Prerequisites (do these before splitting; all worthwhile regardless)

- Fix the reminders leak: tools/_services/plaid_link.py -> penny.reminders -> penny.api.persistence. Post-split this is a circular dependency that won't install. (fly-10q)
- Fix the _services reaches from front doors via the declared facade. (fly-161)
- Move adapters/clerk website-side. (fly-uvz)
- Package-data plumbing (the sneaky one): .prompts/ (promptorium root), .agent/skills/ (SkillRegistry.load(project_root=backend/)), configs/taxonomy.yaml are all repo-layout-relative. As an installed wheel they must load via importlib.resources or a configured root. Until then the agent package only works from a source checkout.
- Add the missing guardrail: penny/adapters/db stays finance+tenancy-only (no app models), so schema ownership is enforced.

## Public/private and IP caveat

Agent public + website private = classic open-core; outsiders get a self-hostable finance agent. Caveat: the system prompt and skills are arguably the secret sauce and would be public; if that matters, prompts could load from an overridable root with only baseline versions published (adds seam complexity — decide if the IP concern is real first).

## Ongoing cost + cheaper variant

Every seam change becomes a two-repo dance (change agent repo -> tag/release -> bump pin -> integrate); atomic cross-domain commits and the single verification gate are lost. Lighter path with the same public/private outcome: keep the monorepo private as source of truth, make the agent a proper installable package in-tree (the prerequisites above), and publish a read-only public mirror of the agent package via git filter-repo/subtree in CI. Atomic commits and one test gate retained; graduating to a true two-repo split later reuses all the same seam work.

## Interaction with the sandbox plan

The Modal sandbox runner (plans/smartplan-modal-sandbox-agent) imports only agent-harness + its own protocol, so it could be a third repo or live in the agent repo; the MCP tool server and Modal lifecycle stay website-side and import the agent package's toolsets — direction preserved.

Related: fly-sle (stabilize architecture umbrella).

title: Categorization events log: derive natural-language reason from conversation context
description: When a recategorization occurs, capture a natural-language reason for the change derived from the surrounding conversation context (why the user wanted it changed, what mistake was being corrected, etc.) and write it to the categorization events log alongside the structured fields.

title: Refactor report flow into core agent_run service
description: Move thick run/report orchestration out of ui/cli.py into core service modules. Establish agent-run as canonical execution path with report as compatibility alias.
acceptance_criteria: Core orchestration lives outside cli.py; cli.py only parses args and delegates. report command remains functional as alias over shared core path.

title: Create service-layer agent_run package and request/result types
description: Add src/transactoid/services/agent_run/ with types.py, service.py, pipeline.py scaffolding and explicit DTOs for run requests, outputs, artifacts, and manifests.
acceptance_criteria: New service package exists with typed request/result structures used by callers; no business logic added to cli.py.

title: Implement core AgentRunService execution flow
description: Implement prompt resolution (prompt vs prompt_key), template variable injection, agent creation/execution, and result extraction in core service.
acceptance_criteria: Service executes runs from --prompt and --prompt-key paths, returns typed results, and is independently invokable without cli-specific code.

title: Implement output pipeline for md/html + target fanout
description: Implement completion pipeline to generate markdown/html artifacts and route outputs to selected targets (r2 and/or local), including local-dir handling and defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Defaults save md+html to r2; local target writes files into configured hidden directory; target and format toggles are respected.

title: Add R2 trace artifact persistence and continuation
description: Persist trace sqlite and run manifest to R2, add R2 download helper(s), and support resume via run-id with parent/child run chaining.
acceptance_criteria: Each run stores trace.sqlite3 and manifest.json in R2; continuation by run-id restores prior trace/session and publishes a new run with parent_run_id.

title: Add thin CLI command transactoid agent-run
description: Add agent-run command in cli.py as a thin adapter: parse flags, build request object, call AgentRunService, print concise outcomes and exit codes.
acceptance_criteria: CLI supports --prompt/--prompt-key/--continue, repeatable --email, output format toggles, output-target fanout, local-dir, and max-turns; orchestration remains outside cli.py.

title: Refactor report command into compatibility alias
description: Make transactoid report delegate into AgentRunService/agent-run path with spending-report preset behavior; remove duplicated report orchestration logic from cli.py/jobs/report where possible.
acceptance_criteria: transactoid report behavior remains intact while reusing shared core service; report-specific duplication in cli.py is removed.

title: Add tests for service, adapter, and CLI alias behavior
description: Add/adjust tests for AgentRunService, output pipeline, R2 download+trace continuation, agent-run CLI parsing/delegation, and report alias delegation.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover prompt and prompt-key runs, continuation from run-id, output target matrix, and report alias wiring; test suite passes.

title: Track memory of each categorization change more diligently
description:
Follow-on to the agentic categorizer (fly-i1e): make the categorizer record durable memory of every categorization change — what changed, why, and the derived rule — so future runs learn from past corrections. Extends the merchant-rules / analysis-memory work (fly-27).

Scope (merged from fly-22, fly-60s, fly-9sw):
- Persist ALL recategorization actions with consistent context; expose retrieval for subsequent recategorization flows; cover with deterministic tests and document (fly-22).
- On each recategorization, capture a natural-language reason derived from the surrounding conversation context (why the user wanted it changed, what was being corrected) and write it to the categorization events log alongside the structured fields (fly-60s).
- Build analysis over the events log to surface patterns: frequently recategorized merchants, common miscategorizations, candidate merchant rules to add, and signals for taxonomy gaps. Output should be actionable, e.g. suggested merchant rules (fly-9sw).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Every categorization/recategorization change persists durable memory (what/why/derived rule) with retrieval available to later runs. 2) NL reason captured from conversation context and stored in the events log with structured fields. 3) Events-log analysis surfaces actionable patterns (recategorized merchants, miscategorizations, candidate rules, taxonomy gaps). 4) Deterministic tests + docs.

title: Ensure skills implementation parity with official agent skills spec
description: Placeholder: audit current skills implementation against the official agent skills spec and document gaps plus required changes.

title: Add skills support to the Claude runtime
description: Placeholder: implement and validate runtime support for skills execution in the Claude runtime path.

title: Complete merchant-rules implementation as a skill
description: Implement and finalize merchant-rules as a reusable skill, including integration points, documentation, and validation of end-to-end behavior.
close_reason: Completed via merchant-rules-redo branch merge to main

title: Give categorizer access to events log, merchant rules, and existing tags
description: Ensure the categorizer prompt/context includes: (1) prior categorization events so past corrections inform future runs; (2) merchant rules from .transactoid/memory/merchant-rules.md (confirm wired); (3) existing tags, so it can decide whether a transaction should also receive an active tag (e.g., 'europe trip 2026' for travel-period expenses). Apply these signals during categorization and tagging.

title: Analyze categorization events log for patterns and derived rules
description: Build analysis over the categorization events log to surface patterns: frequently recategorized merchants, common miscategorizations, candidate merchant rules to add, and signals for taxonomy gaps. Output should be actionable (e.g., suggested merchant rules).

title: Live-resume an in-flight sandboxed turn across a Fly restart (run_id recovery)
description:
## Context

Sandboxed chat turns are now decoupled from the browser connection (commits
577f4fd / 41880ff):

- **Persistence (push):** on `RunEnd` the runner POSTs its whole event log to
  `POST /api/chat/{id}/finalize` (auth = the per-turn MCP capability token),
  retrying until Fly acks. This is **already restart-safe** — the runner holds
  the result and keeps retrying, so the turn persists even if Fly restarts
  mid-turn.
- **Display resume:** `GET /api/chat/{id}/stream` reconnects a browser to an
  in-flight turn via `relay_turn(tunnel_url, run_id)` → the runner's
  `/runs/{run_id}/events`. The `(tunnel_url, run_id, household_id)` mapping lives
  in Fly memory (`sandbox_wiring._active`).

## The gap

After a **Fly restart mid-turn**, `_active` is wiped. Fly can recover the box
(the sandbox is tagged `conversation_id` in Modal — see the reaper work), but it
does **not** know the `run_id`, which keys the runner's events endpoint. So a
browser that reconnects to a turn spanning the restart can't get **live
token-by-token streaming**.

This is deliberately deferred: it's marginal. The turn still persists (push +
retry), and the browser degrades gracefully — it shows the pending state and, on
the next hydrate after the finalize lands, the complete result. Only *live*
streaming across the restart is lost, only when a browser is connected during a
Fly restart mid-turn.

## If we ever want it

- **run_id recovery:** since it's strictly one run per sandbox, add a runner
  endpoint that exposes the current run — `GET /status` returning the active
  run_id (+ idle info), or a `GET /current/events` keyed by conversation instead
  of run_id. Then resume needs only the box (from the Modal `conversation_id`
  tag), not a Fly-side run_id.
- **Frontend restart-recovery:** when `resumeStream()` 204s while the client
  still believes a turn is in flight, fall back to polling `GET /api/sessions`
  until the assistant turn is persisted (then render it), rather than sitting on
  the pending state. Optionally retry `resumeStream()` a few times first in case
  Fly is momentarily reconnecting.

## Not required for

Persistence durability (already handled) or resume across an ordinary
disconnect (works via in-memory `_active`). Related to the reaper follow-up,
which uses the same `conversation_id` tag + persisted-last-message idle
detection.

title: Audit and clean up taxonomy
description: Audit the two-level category taxonomy (configs/taxonomy.yaml) for inconsistencies and tighten naming conventions. Examples: prefer one-word names over two-word names where possible, normalize casing/punctuation, consolidate near-duplicates, and ensure parent/child names follow a consistent style across the tree.

title: Run agent runs on a separate machine (sprites or Runloop)
description: Move agent runs off the request-serving host onto dedicated compute. Evaluate sprites or Runloop (or similar) for isolated, on-demand agent execution. Goal: scalable, sandboxed agent runs decoupled from the web/backend process.

title: Fix create_all vs alembic drift once and for all
description:
Prod schema is effectively create_all-managed, not alembic-managed: the schema is built at runtime by DB.create_schema() (Base.metadata.create_all), while alembic_version lagged behind (stuck at 006 while later tables/columns already existed via create_all). This split-brain keeps biting us — create_all adds missing TABLES but never missing COLUMNS, the alembic history has had divergent heads, and applying a single migration to prod requires stamp gymnastics.

Goal: make alembic the single source of truth for schema evolution and stop relying on create_all in managed environments, once and for all.

Likely scope:
- Reconcile alembic_version with the real prod schema (stamp to the true current head so the graph matches reality; resolve any remaining multi-head).
- Author a baseline/catch-up migration for any columns/objects that create_all introduced but no migration covers (e.g. is_hidden, eval_runs, original_descriptor, plaid raw_name/counterparties/personal_finance_category — verify current state before asserting).
- Change bootstrap so create_all is a DEV-ONLY convenience (SQLite/local) and managed DBs (Neon prod + test) evolve via 'alembic upgrade head' only — no create_all in prod startup.
- Make 'alembic upgrade head' from a clean checkout succeed against a fresh DB (single head, no re-create-existing-object failures).
- Document the migration workflow so new columns always go through a migration.

Do prod experiments on a scratch branch off production (direct ALTER is permission-gated); delete the scratch branch after. See memory project-prod-alembic-create-all.
acceptance_criteria:
- alembic_version reflects the real schema; single head; 'alembic upgrade head' succeeds from a clean state against a fresh DB.
- No schema-relevant object exists in prod that lacks a migration (create_all no longer silently owns any table/column).
- Managed DBs (prod + test) evolve via alembic only; create_all is confined to dev/SQLite convenience.
- Migration workflow documented; a new column added via migration flows cleanly to prod.

title: Properly host the web app and backend
description:
Get the web frontend and FastAPI backend properly hosted in a deployable environment (not just localhost). Builds on the deploy pipeline already merged. Includes the Plaid Link redirect rearchitecture (productionization B-6) needed for remote hosting, since connect_new_account currently only works on the user's machine.

Also covers reviving and modernizing ChatKit web support (merged from fly-33): re-enable the ChatKit web surface, align it with current runtime/tooling interfaces, and bring it to parity with the non-web interaction paths. End state: a hosted web experience where core interaction flows (session start, message exchange, tool/event rendering) work end-to-end against the deployed backend.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Web frontend + FastAPI backend are hosted in a real deployable environment. 2) Plaid Link redirect rearchitecture (B-6) works for remote hosting. 3) ChatKit web support is functional end-to-end: session start, message exchange, tool/event rendering. 4) Tests/validation cover critical hosted-web ChatKit paths.

title: Convert categorizer from single LLM call to an agent
description:
Replace the single-shot LLM categorization call with an agentic categorizer that can reason in multiple steps, use tools (lookups against taxonomy, merchant rules, events log, prior decisions), and iterate. Goal: higher-fidelity categorization than a one-shot prompt. Implementation lives under tools/_services/ (categorizer).

Merged from fly-9j9 — the categorizer's prompt/context must include: (1) prior categorization events so past corrections inform future runs; (2) merchant rules from .transactoid/memory/merchant-rules.md (confirm wired); (3) existing tags, so it can decide whether a transaction should also receive an active tag (e.g. 'europe trip 2026' for travel-period expenses). Apply these signals during both categorization and tagging.

title: Switch Clerk to production instance
description:
Move Clerk auth from the development instance to a production instance for the Penny web app.

Domain: website/app + deploy. Clerk is auth — a cross-cutting provider consumed by the website domain; its keys/config cross the boundary only through the PENNY_* env/config contract that deploy supplies.

Likely scope:
- Create/configure the Clerk production instance (production API keys, allowed origins/redirects, production domain, any required DNS/CNAME for Clerk's production Frontend API).
- Swap dev -> prod publishable/secret keys via the env contract (config.py + PENNY_* vars); update .env.example and deploy-supplied values (fly [env]/secrets), not hardcoded anywhere.
- Verify sign-in/sign-up and getToken() flows work end-to-end against the prod instance in the deployed app.
acceptance_criteria:
- Clerk production instance is configured (keys, domains, redirects, DNS as needed).
- App reads Clerk keys through the PENNY_* env/config contract; no dev keys or topology hardcoded in app code.
- Deploy supplies prod keys via fly env/secrets; .env.example updated.
- Auth (sign-in/sign-up + token) verified working against the prod instance in the deployed web app.

title: Overall financial health dashboard
description: Build a dashboard summarizing overall financial health: spending vs. income, savings rate, category breakdowns, net worth trend, debt levels, key ratios. Single-pane view for at-a-glance status.

title: Support document uploading via the agent UI
description:
Add support for uploading documents (files) through the agent UI, wired end-to-end with R2 for storage.

Scope spans two domains:
- agent-ui package (@adambossy/agent-ui): add upload affordance to the chat surface (Composer/Message) so users can attach/upload documents in the conversation, following the Vercel AI SDK UI message-stream protocol for file/attachment parts.
- Penny web app implementation: wire the upload to R2 via the website domain (FastAPI surface + storage adapter). Uploads are a website/app concern (user-facing CRUD + persistence), so they live in website-owned code, not the agent domain.

Storage backend: Cloudflare R2 (existing penny.adapters.storage).

Segregation notes (per AGENTS.local.md HARD CONSTRAINT):
- The upload endpoint + persistence is website/app domain (backend/penny/api/), Website -> Agent only.
- Do not put upload/app CRUD into the finance facade penny/adapters/db; app-owned data uses the website's own store/schema.
- R2 is a cross-cutting connector reached through the storage adapter.
design:
Two-part delivery:
1. agent-ui: upload UI + attachment message parts (upstream package change in ~/code/agent-ui).
2. web app: R2-backed upload endpoint in the FastAPI surface + storage adapter usage; associate uploaded docs with the conversation/user via website persistence.
acceptance_criteria:
- A user can upload a document from the agent UI chat surface.
- The document is stored in R2 via the website/storage adapter.
- Upload flows through the website domain only (no agent-domain imports of website persistence).
- Works against the deployed web app implementation, not just locally.

title: agent-ui: document upload UI + attachment message parts
description:
In @adambossy/agent-ui (~/code/agent-ui), add the upload affordance to the chat surface so users can attach/upload documents in a conversation.

- Add an attach/upload control to the Composer.
- Render uploaded documents as attachment/file message parts in Message, per the Vercel AI SDK UI message-stream protocol.
- Emit file/attachment parts the host app can wire to its own upload backend (the package stays backend-agnostic — it does not talk to R2 directly).

This is an upstream package change; Penny consumes it via the vite alias to ~/code/agent-ui.
acceptance_criteria:
- Composer exposes a document upload affordance.
- Uploaded documents render as attachment parts in the message stream.
- The package remains storage-agnostic; the host supplies the upload handler.

title: web app: R2-backed document upload endpoint + persistence
description:
Wire the agent-ui upload affordance to Cloudflare R2 in the Penny web app.

- Add an upload endpoint to the FastAPI surface (backend/penny/api/) that accepts a document and stores it in R2 via penny.adapters.storage.
- Persist the document->conversation/user association in the website's own store (NOT the finance penny/adapters/db facade), per the agent/website segregation HARD CONSTRAINT.
- Supply the upload handler to agent-ui from the frontend so attachments post to this endpoint.

Domain: website/app (Website -> Agent only). R2 reached through the storage adapter (cross-cutting connector).
acceptance_criteria:
- Frontend upload handler posts documents to the web app endpoint.
- Endpoint stores the document in R2 via the storage adapter.
- Document metadata/association persists in website-owned storage, not the finance DB facade.
- No agent-domain code imports website persistence (segregation guardrail holds).

title: Add Langfuse tracing
description: Instrument the agent and categorizer with Langfuse tracing so every run, tool call, and LLM call is captured for inspection. Wire config via PENNY_* env vars and ensure traces flow from both the chat agent and scheduled/cron runs.

comments.author: adambossy
comments.text: Implemented in worktree feat+langfuse-tracing (branch worktree-feat+langfuse-tracing). Added penny.observability (Langfuse v4 SDK): agent loop traced via a 2nd EventBus subscriber wired into api/bridge.py (chat) and cli.py _drive_agent (cron); categorizer traced via categorizer_span + llm_generation context managers covering both Gemini and OpenAI calls with token usage. Config: LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY/HOST + PENNY_LANGFUSE_ENABLED/ENVIRONMENT (.env.example updated). Strict no-op when unconfigured. 5 new tests via in-memory OTEL exporter; full suite (149) + ruff green. Follow-up: PII masking (transaction data) not yet wired.

comments.author: adambossy
comments.text: Refactored to the vendor-neutral end-state. agent-harness (branch feat/otel-tracing-subscriber): implemented the real OTELSubscriber in tracing/otel.py — an EventBus subscriber emitting GenAI-semconv spans (invoke_agent/chat/execute_tool), lazy opentelemetry import so tracing/ still imports without the [otel] extra; replaced skeleton test (706 unit tests green). Penny: dropped the langfuse SDK; penny.observability now configures an OTEL TracerProvider + OTLP exporter to Langfuse ({HOST}/api/public/otel/v1/traces, Basic auth) and just attaches agent-harness's OTELSubscriber (no agent-loop translation code in Penny anymore). Categorizer traced via raw-OTEL categorizer_span/llm_generation. Deps: agent-harness[...,otel] + opentelemetry-sdk + opentelemetry-exporter-otlp-proto-http. Verified end-to-end: real spans POST to Langfuse US OTLP endpoint => SpanExportResult.SUCCESS. 148 Penny tests green. NOTE: to ship, agent-harness needs its branch committed + the git pin in Penny pyproject bumped (currently using the [tool.uv.sources] editable local path).

title: Allow renaming merchant descriptor on transactions
description:
Add the ability to change the merchant descriptor for a transaction (or group of transactions sharing a useless descriptor). Motivation: opaque descriptors like 'Venmo', 'Zelle', or 'SQ *' carry no signal — letting the user (or agent) assign a proper merchant name unlocks correct categorization and reporting.

Requirements:
- Persist the original descriptor alongside the rename so we never lose the source-of-truth Plaid value.
- Record the rename in an events log so the history is auditable. Open question: extend the existing categorization events log vs. introduce a dedicated merchant-rename log. Decide based on whether downstream consumers (analytics, categorizer context) benefit from a unified vs. split feed.
- Surface renames to the categorizer as input context, so future categorization decisions for the same descriptor (or merchant) reflect the user's chosen name.
- Expose via MCP tool + CLI, mirroring the recategorize_merchant pattern.
- Consider whether a rename should also produce a persistent rule (similar to merchant-rules.md) so future syncs auto-apply the rename.

title: Convert the sync tool into a continuous ingestion pipeline
description: Replace the on-demand sync tool with a continuous ingestion pipeline that keeps Plaid transactions flowing in automatically rather than only when sync is invoked. Should run on a schedule/stream, handle incremental updates and backfill, dedupe, and feed the categorizer without a manual trigger. Relates to transaction dedupe (fly-38) and moving agent/background work onto separate compute (fly-bdu).

title: Categorizer agent silently leaves transactions uncategorized (regression since PR #49)
description:
## Symptom

Since **2026-06-29** the sync cron creates transactions but does not categorize
them: everything synced Jul 1–5 is `category_id=NULL` / `category_model=NULL`
(~145 rows and counting), in prod and the penny-test copy. Last successful
categorization: 2026-06-29 07:01 (old path).

## Root cause (confirmed)

The break coincides exactly with **PR #49 `d8435c5`** (2026-06-29 22:24) — the
switch from the direct-LLM categorizer to the per-transaction **agent**
(`categorizer_agent.categorize_one`). That work is [[fly-i1e]] ("convert
categorizer to an agent").

The agent path has a **silent-failure** shape: `categorize_one` runs the agent,
then reads back whether it called `submit_categorization`; if it didn't, it
returns `category_key=None` and the row stays uncategorized — **no exception is
raised**, so the sync reports success. So a categorization that never lands is
invisible end-to-end.

Invoking `categorize_one` on a **single** transaction works (verified: a Jul-3
"Variety Coffee Roasters" row categorized correctly). So the code isn't totally
broken — the failure is at **batch scale in the end-of-sync sweep**
(`_categorize_uncategorized` → `_categorize_derived`, many agents concurrent).
Prime suspects, unconfirmed: concurrency/rate-limit/timeout under load, or the
tool setup (the Gemini web-search builtin disables AFC; submission relies on the
harness loop). Note: today's sandbox deploy did NOT touch the categorizer — the
code is byte-identical to old prod, so it is still broken in the current image.

## Done so far

- Wired the categorizer agent to Langfuse (commit `c471aa0`): one
  `penny-categorizer` trace per transaction + `penny sync` flushes on exit.
  Verified a real categorization emits a full trace (AGENT + GENERATIONs + the
  `submit_categorization` TOOL call). This makes the failure diagnosable.

## Next

- Deploy the backend so the cron image carries the traced categorizer, run a
  `penny sync`, and read the `penny-categorizer` traces to see exactly why the
  agents don't submit at scale, then fix.
- Recovery once fixed is free: the end-of-sync sweep is cursor-independent, so
  one good sync re-categorizes the whole backlog.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (reproduced on the prod copy): the categorizer sweep aborted on transient Gemini 503/UNAVAILABLE under concurrency=6 — one 503 failed the whole asyncio.gather, leaving every synced row uncategorized (silent: inserts already committed). The agent itself is fine — 13/13 sampled Langfuse traces that ran to completion called submit_categorization correctly. Also found a SECOND, overlapping outage: penny sync set no tenant context, so post-July-tenancy-cutover the plaid_transactions insert failed household_id NOT NULL and NO transactions synced for ~2 days.

FIX LANDED (commit b10c370): (1) penny sync now iterates one principal per (household,owner) with a pinned context + app-level item scoping; (2) sweep uses gather(return_exceptions=True) + categorize_one retries transient errors + concurrency 6->3. Pending: deploy + a real prod sync to confirm the backlog drains and new transactions categorize.

title: Stabilize architecture and segregate components
description:
Stabilize the architecture and segregate components, building on the feat/account-creation work (branch refactor/stabilize-architecture, based off feat/account-creation).

Goal: converge toward the target layered-domain architecture and enforce the domain-segregation HARD CONSTRAINTS, per AGENTS.md and AGENTS.local.md.

Axes to address:
- Domain segregation (agent vs website vs deploy): keep dependencies one-directional (Website -> Agent, Deploy -> App). No agent-domain imports of website persistence; no app CRUD in the finance penny/adapters/db facade; app data in a website-owned store/schema.
- Layered domains within a domain: Types -> Config -> Repo -> Service -> Runtime -> UI, deps flowing left only. Nudge misplaced responsibilities into their layer.
- Cross-cutting connectors (Plaid, R2, Langfuse, auth) enter through explicit provider seams rather than ad-hoc imports.

Follow the campfire rule: continuous small in-scope refactors, not a big rewrite. Flag misalignments that are out of scope rather than expanding the diff.
design: Work from the feat/account-creation baseline. Identify the sharpest segregation/layering violations touched by that work (account creation likely introduces website/app persistence + auth) and stabilize those boundaries first. Add/tighten guardrail tests for the import boundaries where practical.
acceptance_criteria:
- Component boundaries are clearer and dependency directions hold (agent/website/deploy segregation intact).
- App-owned data lives in a website-owned store, not the finance facade.
- Cross-cutting connectors are reached through provider seams where touched.
- Verification gate passes: ruff check, ruff format --check, pytest.

title: Record Plaid-provided categories and reconcile against ours
description: Persist Plaid's own personal_finance_category / category fields on raw transactions, and add a reconciliation step that compares Plaid's category to ours. Surface mismatches for review and use as a signal during categorization.

title: Financial projection capabilities
description: Project forward-looking financials based on historical data: projected spend for the year (by category and total), projected net worth trajectory, income vs. spend trendlines. Use historical run-rate plus seasonality where applicable.

title: Add account setup flow for MCP users
description: Provide a first-run/account setup flow for users coming in via the MCP. Walk a new MCP user through connecting their data and provisioning their account/workspace so they can go from zero to a usable Penny without manual backend setup. Depends conceptually on account creation.

title: Full net worth calculations
description: Compute total net worth across all connected accounts (assets minus liabilities), including investments, cash, credit, loans. Expose via CLI/MCP and/or dashboard.

title: Move Clerk adapter out of shared penny/adapters/ into website-owned code
description:
# Move the Clerk adapter out of the shared adapters/ umbrella into website-owned code

## Background (why this matters)

`backend/penny/adapters/` is the agent/finance domain's I/O layer — db facade + models, cache, Plaid client, R2 storage, Amazon. Every other package under it is imported by agent-domain code (tools, `_services`, plugins). Per the agent↔website hard constraint (AGENTS.local.md) and the layered-domain target (AGENTS.md "Architecture: layered domains"), website-only concerns should live in website-owned packages, and a reader should be able to treat `penny/adapters/` as "what the finance agent talks to".

## The misfile

`backend/penny/adapters/clerk.py` is **app/website-only**: it wraps the Clerk identity provider (JWT verification, Backend API user fetch, invites). Its importers are exclusively website code:

- `penny/api/auth.py` (~line 26) — JWT verification / identity resolution for `request_context()`.
- `penny/api/signup_routes.py` (~line 20) — `ClerkInvites` for household invites.
- `penny/signup.py` — signup/provisioning orchestration.

No agent-domain module imports it. (Verified on branch `refactor/stabilize-architecture` circa 2026-07; re-grep `adapters.clerk`/`adapters/clerk` to confirm the current importer set before moving.)

Leaving it under the shared umbrella invites two problems: (a) it blurs the "adapters = finance/agent I/O" rule, making it easier for future agent code to import an identity-provider client it must never touch; (b) it would wrongly travel with the agent if the agent domain is ever extracted into its own package.

## Fix approach

- Move the module into website-owned code, e.g. `penny/api/clerk.py` (beside `api/auth.py`, its main consumer) — or, if a website-side adapters grouping is preferred, something like `penny/api/adapters/clerk.py`. Pick whichever reads most consistently with the surrounding api/ package; a flat `penny/api/clerk.py` is the simplest and matches the existing thin-module style there.
- Update the imports in `api/auth.py`, `api/signup_routes.py`, `penny/signup.py` (and any tests referencing the old path).
- Pure mechanical move — do not change behaviour or the module's interface.
- Optional hardening, in the spirit of `tests/test_domain_segregation.py`: assert the agent domain (`penny/tools`, `.agent/skills`, `penny/agent_factory.py`) never imports the Clerk module at its new path. Cheap to add while touching the segregation tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- No `clerk` module remains under `penny/adapters/`; everything under `penny/adapters/` is imported by agent/finance code (or shared infra), not website-only code.
- All previous importers updated; auth, signup, and invite flows behave exactly as before (existing tests pass).
- Verification gate passes: `cd backend && uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check . && uv run pytest -q`.

## Context

Found during an architecture segregation audit (2026-07): the agent↔website import boundary is otherwise clean and CI-guarded; this was one of three boundary items (the others: a transitive agent→web-store crossing via `penny.reminders`, and front doors importing `penny.tools._services` directly — separate beads).

title: Link email reports to categorization-fix runs with the fix prompt seeded
description:
In the spending/email reports, add actionable links/buttons that open the hosted web experience and continue an agent run pre-seeded to fix a categorization. Clicking through opens a continued agent session in the web app with the fix prompt already populated so the user can correct a category in one step.

Merged from fly-35: generate signed/deep-linkable actions in report emails that map to predefined prompt intents and resume/continue the relevant agent context in the hosted web ChatKit flow. Links must be secure/validated and degrade gracefully on expired/invalid state. Depends on the hosted web experience (fly-fhm).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Report emails include continuation buttons for predefined follow-up prompts (incl. categorization fixes). 2) Clicking a button opens the hosted web experience and continues the run with the intended fix prompt pre-populated. 3) Links are signed/validated and handle expired/invalid states gracefully. 4) Tests cover link generation and continuation behavior.

title: Add account creation
description: Implement account creation so the app supports real user accounts. Today Penny is single-user with no auth (see plans/20260524-224025-productionize-transactoid.md). Add the ability to create an account as the first step toward multi-user support: account record/model, creation entry point, and the workspace/data wiring that currently assumes a single implicit user.

title: Revisit warm-process restore via Modal memory snapshots at GA
description:
Memory snapshots at GA (forward-looking).

If Modal ships non-expiring sandbox memory snapshots, restore-with-warm-process becomes possible and the cold-start budget shrinks. The current disk-rehydration design keeps this additive — i.e. we can layer warm-process restore on top without reworking the disk-rehydration path.

Affects: Image bake.

Action when Modal GAs non-expiring memory snapshots: evaluate restoring a warm process from a snapshot (instead of / in addition to disk rehydration), and fold it into the Image bake step to cut cold-start latency. Track Modal's snapshot GA status; this is deferred until that lands.
acceptance_criteria:
- When Modal non-expiring memory snapshots reach GA, warm-process restore is evaluated against the current disk-rehydration approach.
- If adopted, it's layered additively (disk-rehydration path preserved) and integrated into Image bake.
- Cold-start budget impact measured.

title: Show conversation history in the Penny chat UI
description:
Surface past conversations in the Penny chat UI so a user can see and revisit their conversation history (not just the current live session).

Domain: website/app. Conversation/message persistence is owned by the website domain (backend/penny/api/), and the history UI is consumed from the frontend. Reads go through website-owned persistence — the agent domain is not involved in listing/loading history.

Likely scope:
- Frontend: a history surface (list of past conversations) + ability to open one and render its messages via the existing agent-ui Message components.
- Web app: endpoints to list conversations and fetch a conversation's messages from website-owned persistence.
acceptance_criteria:
- The chat UI displays a list of the user's past conversations.
- Selecting a past conversation loads and renders its message history.
- History is read from website-owned persistence; no agent-domain involvement.
- Works against the deployed web app, not just locally.
